layer_1_flower.intent: Euphoric ease wrapped in dark fruit velvet
layer_1_flower.lineage: (Forbidden Fruit X Watermelon Z) X Blueprint

layer_2_essential_oil.blend_rationale: Black Pepper carries the dominant beta-caryophyllene. Lemon matches the candied plum aroma note. Sage provides α-Humulene support. Lavender rounds the blend with a soft finish.

layer_3_music.spotify_prompt: A mid-energy session. Euphoric ease wrapped in dark fruit velvet. Genres: indie rock, neo-soul, jazz, alternative. Tempo around 85-110 BPM.

layer_4_tea.name: Forbidden Tea
layer_4_tea.description: Candied Plum and maple syrup infused oolong blend
method: Heat 8 oz filtered water to 195°F.
Add oolong tea bag and steep for 3-4 minutes.
Add umeboshi syrup and let infuse for 1 minute.
Add maple syrup and let infuse for 1 minute.
Strain into a warm teacup.
Stir in honey and serve.

mocktail.name: Forbidden Fizz
mocktail.description: Candied Plum and maple syrup refresher
method: Fill a rocks glass with large ice cubes.
Combine 2 oz plum shrub and 0.75 oz fresh lemon juice in a shaker with ice.
Add 0.5 oz maple syrup.
Shake for 8 seconds.
Strain into glass over fresh ice.
Top with 2 oz sparkling water and stir gently.
Garnish with candied plum.

cocktail.name: Forbidden Spritz
cocktail.description: Gin-based candied plum and maple syrup cocktail
method: Combine 2 oz gin, 1.5 oz plum shrub, and 0.75 oz fresh lemon juice in a shaker.
Add 0.5 oz maple syrup.
Add ice.
Add ice and shake for 10 seconds.
Strain into a chilled highball.
Garnish with candied plum.

juice.name: Forbidden Juice
method: Press 2 cups fresh plum through a cold press.
Press 1 cup apple through the press.
Add 0.5 inch fresh ginger root to the press.
Combine all juices in a glass.
Add 0.5 oz raw honey and stir until dissolved.
Pour over ice and serve immediately.

wellness_shot.name: Forbidden Shot
method: Chill a shot glass in the freezer for 2 minutes.
In a mixing glass, combine 1 oz gin with 0.75 oz fresh plum juice.
Add 0.25 oz fresh lemon juice.
Stir with ice for 5 seconds.
Strain into the chilled shot glass and serve immediately.

snack.name: Spiced Cashews with Lime & Chili
snack.desc: Roasted cashews with lime zest, chili flakes, and smoked paprika.

appetizer.name: Ahi Tuna Poke Bowl
appetizer.desc: Fresh ahi tuna with avocado, sesame, ponzu, and crispy shallots.

main.name: Pan-Seared Salmon with Herb Butter
main.desc: Wild-caught salmon with compound butter of dill, lemon, and capers over wilted greens.

dessert.name: Lemon Olive Oil Cake
dessert.desc: Moist cake with Meyer lemon, local olive oil, and a dusting of powdered sugar.

cheese.name: Fresh Mozzarella & Heirloom Tomatoes
cheese.desc: Hand-pulled mozzarella with heirloom tomatoes, basil oil, and aged balsamic.

bath_bomb.base: baking soda, citric acid, cornstarch, Epsom salt
bath_bomb.extras: dried flower petals, oat milk powder

shower_steamer.activation: Place on shower floor away from direct stream. Steam activates essential oils for 5-10 minutes of aromatherapy.

layer_10_incense.burn_method: Place on heat-resistant surface in a ventilated room. Allow 30 seconds for the flame to establish before extinguishing to a smolder.
layer_10_incense.ritual_suggestion: Light before the session begins. Let the smoke settle the room while you prepare your euphoric space.

layer_11_environment.spatial_setup: Mixed seating heights, conversation-friendly arrangement. Ambient background music visible.
layer_11_environment.scent_anchor: Essential oil blend via diffuser, started 25 minutes before session

layer_12_breathwork.timing: Begin with the first sip. Alternate breathing cycles with mindful tasting.
layer_12_breathwork.guidance_text: Find your center. Breathe in through the nose for four counts. Hold for four, noticing the stillness. Exhale slowly through the mouth for six counts, letting the breath carry tension away.

layer_13_narrative.text:
19 drops of black pepper carry the dominant β-Caryophyllene through the room steadily — the way Alpenglow Farms intended when they cultivated Forbidden Blueprint in Benbow, Humboldt County. The lineage reads (Forbidden Fruit X Watermelon Z) X Blueprint, but what arrives in the glass is something that transcends parentage: candied plum, maple syrup, and raspberry.

The second oil — Lemon — adds 10 drops of brightness that matches the maple syrup note in the flower itself. Relaxed is not a command here but an invitation, the way the best strains suggest rather than insist. The body balances into euphoric with the kind of molecular precision that only terpenes at 2.5% total can deliver.

In the warm gathering space, the essential oil blend has been working for thirty minutes before the flower arrives. The pre-load creates a terpene environment that the cannabis completes rather than initiates — your olfactory system already tuned to the frequency of β-Caryophyllene, already primed for euphoric ease wrapped in dark fruit velvet. The third oil adds depth. The fourth oil rounds everything into a finish that feels inevitable rather than constructed.

This is what Alpenglow Farms grows in Benbow, Humboldt County: not just cannabis, but context. The hybrid genetics express through terpenes that speak the same chemical language as bay laurel on a Humboldt hillside. The session balances into its final form — comfortable, unhurried, complete — and the 4 oils that started it are now indistinguishable from the flower that finished it.

layer_14_location.terpene_walk: Begin at SF Botanical Garden — Fragrance Garden, diffuse essential oil blend 30 minutes before arrival. Walk slowly, breathing deeply through the nose to layer forest phytoncides over the pre-loaded terpene profile.

wine.terpene_bridge: Rotundone in Syrah shares the spicy, peppery character of β-caryophyllene — same aromatic family, different kingdoms.
wine.tasting_note: Dark fruit, cracked pepper, coastal minerality

wine.terpene_bridge: Zinfandel's brambly spice and warm pepper notes echo caryophyllene's warming, anti-inflammatory character.

beer.terpene_bridge: Belgian yeast esters produce spicy, peppery phenolics that echo caryophyllene's warming quality.
Centennial hops carry significant caryophyllene — cannabis and hops are Cannabaceae cousins.

layer_15_wine_beer.cannabaceae_note: Cannabis and hops (Humulus lupulus) are botanical cousins in the family Cannabaceae, sharing terpene synthesis pathways that produce identical aromatic molecules.
